breadth.note: 4 of 11 sectors Leading on 1m RS (XLV +5.2, XLF +4.9, XLU +3.6, XLI +3.5) — the defensive core NARROWED as XLP dropped OUT of Leading (+0.5→−0.6, now Weakening) and XLY deepened to Lagging (−0.7→−1.8). The signal delta: XLK relative-strength kept HEALING (1m RS −0.59→−0.53, 3m +18.8→+20.3) — a FOURTH consecutive improvement off the −6.15 pre-market low, now hovering just below a positive cross — WHILE XLF (+4.2→+4.9), XLI (+2.5→+3.5) and XLU (+1.6→+3.6) STRENGTHENED. That is a cyclical + rate-sensitive broadening, not a pure defensive bid. Sharp deterioration: XLE collapsed (−4.2→−6.1, the worst 1m mover — a proxy the crude spike is fading) and XLC deepened to −3.9 (worst laggard). Proxy from sector-member bars, not an exchange breadth feed.

regime.note: PRE-MARKET = the 'shakeout resolved up, rotation turning back to tech' thesis EXTENDED overnight into a genuine GAP-UP. The momentum/tech complex gapped up hard (QQQ +1.34%, SPMO +2.88%, VGT +1.83%) with 5-day momentum healing to ~flat (QQQ −3.39→−0.58, SPMO −6.79→−0.27) and VGT's 5-day FLIPPING POSITIVE (−3.39→+0.31) — the 'add on the 5-day cross' trigger the prior runs flagged is now HITTING. VIX ticked down to 16.74 (low, calm) = the gap is orderly / positive-gamma, not a fear spike. Under the hood the rotation is a leadership HANDOFF, not a narrow bounce: XLK RS healed a fourth straight read toward a 0-cross while XLF/XLI/XLU strengthened (cyclicals + rate-sensitives broadening) and staples (XLP) dropped OUT of Leading. Two tells cut against a clean all-clear: (1) MSFT DIVERGED DOWN −1.78% overnight, its 5-day rolling negative (−2.02) — the mega-cap software laggard did NOT join the gap, a breadth divergence inside the complex; (2) the gap itself invites a morning gap-fade. Energy collapsing (XLE −6.05) reads as crude fading — which would REMOVE the headline-CPI tail (a bullish falsifier of the inflation worry). Falsifiers: the gap fully FILLS and the complex reverses red intraday (dead-cat gap); XLK 1m RS rolling back below −0.6; a 30y auction TAIL today re-firing yields; a hot June CPI (7/14). The read leans 'leadership handoff to tech/cyclicals underway' over 'exhaustion gap to fade' — but MSFT's divergence + the 8:30am claims / 1pm auction keep it two-sided into the open.

attention.title: Overnight GAP-UP — QQQ/SPMO/VGT gapped up, 5-day momentum healed to ~flat, VGT's 5-day crossed POSITIVE
attention.what: The complex gapped up off the 7/8 close: QQQ 711.44→720.95 (+1.34%), SPMO 150.57→154.90 (+2.88%), VGT 115.47→117.58 (+1.83%). 5-day momentum snapped back toward flat (QQQ −3.39→−0.58, SPMO −6.79→−0.27) and VGT's 5-day FLIPPED POSITIVE (−3.39→+0.31). VIX ticked down to 16.74. Est. from Tier-A pre-market bars.
attention.why: The 'add on the 5-day cross' trigger the prior runs flagged is now HITTING: VGT already crossed >0; QQQ (−0.58) and SPMO (−0.27) are a whisker away, all with 21/63-day trend intact (QQQ +21, SPMO +29 on 63d). The gap came with VIX DOWN — orderly / positive-gamma, not a fear chase. This flips the posture from 'add on confirmation' to 'confirmation is arriving' — but a fresh gap invites a morning gap-fade, so the level to hold is the gap base, not the print.

attention.title: Leadership HANDOFF: XLK RS healed a 4th read toward a 0-cross while XLF/XLI/XLU strengthen and staples drop out
attention.what: XLK 1m RS −0.59→−0.53 (4th straight improvement off the −6.15 pre-market low), 3m RS +18.8→+20.3 — hovering just below a positive cross. Simultaneously XLF +4.2→+4.9, XLI +2.5→+3.5, XLU +1.6→+3.6 all STRENGTHENED, while XLP dropped OUT of Leading (+0.5→−0.6) and XLY deepened to Lagging (−0.7→−1.8). Est. from sector bars.
attention.why: This is the most actionable rotation delta: it is no longer a pure defensive bid — the market is broadening into cyclicals (financials/industrials) + rate-sensitives (utilities) WHILE the deepest laggard (tech) closes on a positive RS cross and staples/discretionary bleed. The rotate/re-entry trigger is XLK 1m RS crossing >0 (now −0.53) confirmed by QQQ holding its gap + reclaiming its 50-day; the negate is XLK rolling back below −0.6.

attention.title: MSFT DIVERGED DOWN −1.8% overnight — the mega-cap software laggard did NOT join the gap
attention.what: While the complex gapped up, MSFT fell 383.34→376.53 (−1.78%), its 5-day rolling NEGATIVE (+2.77→−2.02), 21-day still −8.55, 63-day fading to +0.81 (from +3.19). It is now the lone red name in the watchlist and the only one whose 5-day deteriorated. Est. from watchlist bars.
attention.why: A breadth divergence INSIDE the tech complex: the RS heal is being led by semis/broad-tech (VGT/SPMO/QQQ), not mega-cap software. MSFT's counter-trend bounce (5d had been +2.77) failed overnight, leaving it a pure event trade into the 7/29 FOMC-day print, not a momentum long. If MSFT weakness spreads to the software cohort it caps how far the XLK RS cross can carry — watch whether QQQ/VGT can hold the gap without the largest tech weight participating.

attention.title: Energy COLLAPSED (XLE 1m RS −6.05, worst mover) — a crude-fade proxy that eases the headline-CPI tail
attention.what: XLE 1m RS −4.2→−6.05 — the single worst 1m RS in the sector board and the sharpest one-run deterioration (3m −20.3→−15.1 is a base effect, not strength). The carried crude ~$74 (+5.1%, STALE from 7/8) is now CONTRADICTED by the energy tape rolling over. Proxy: energy weakness ≈ crude fading; re-verify WTI live.
attention.why: If crude is mean-reverting lower, it REMOVES the headline-CPI tail the prior runs kept live into 7/14 — a bullish falsifier of the inflation worry that also fits the risk-on gap-up + easing-yields read (utilities bid, tech bid, energy sold). Don't chase XLE for TREND on the way down; treat any long-energy as a shrinking CPI-tail hedge, not a rotation. Trend re-entry only if WTI holds a level AND XLE 1m RS crosses back >0.

attention.title: IMMINENT (today): 7/9 jobless claims 8:30am (cons ~218k) + 30y bond auction 1pm — the in-window catalysts
attention.what: Carried/web-verified (BLS/Treasury): weekly jobless claims TODAY 8:30am ET, consensus ~218k vs ~215k prior; 30y bond auction TODAY 1:00pm ET — following the 7/8 10y that stopped ~4.58% (a second consecutive long-end supply test). 10y ~4.55-4.58% carried. Consensus is an estimate — confirm at the release.
attention.why: The nearest live catalysts and the first tests of the gap-up: claims lands ~1h after this window and an hour before the open, a 30y auction at 1pm. A soft 30y (tail = long-end supply indigestion) would lift yields and re-pressure the long-duration tech that just gapped up; a hot claims print cuts rate-cut odds. A clean auction + benign claims lets the leadership handoff keep building — the base case, but the auction tail is the underrated intraday risk before CPI (7/14).

attention.title: The macro cluster: CPI 7/14 + PPI 7/15 + retail sales 7/16 into OPEX 7/17 — the near-term decision fork
attention.what: Carried/web-verified (BLS/Census): June CPI 7/14 8:30am, June PPI 7/15 8:30am, June RETAIL SALES 7/16 8:30am, then OPEX 7/17 — four catalysts in four sessions, and CPI-week is the Q2 big-bank earnings kickoff (XLF read-through). CPI consensus ~+0.2-0.3% m/m is an ESTIMATE — confirm live. The headline-CPI tail is EASING vs prior runs as energy/crude fades.
attention.why: This cluster governs whether the leadership handoff extends or reverses: with the oil tail fading (XLE collapse), a cool/in-line CPI is now the more likely path and the squeeze fuel for the momentum complex already gapping up; a hot core print re-fires the tech de-rate and re-empowers defensives. PPI confirms/fades CPI a day later; retail sales tests the XLY consumer read (already Lagging). All three land 1-3 sessions before the OPEX pin.

attention.title: SPY gamma levels are now 3 sessions STALE (Jul-6) — re-pull before trusting the flip or the OPEX pin
attention.what: Last-known SPY structure (2026-07-06, STALE): flip ~740, call wall ~750, put wall ~720, ATH 760.4, IV-rank ~15. No fresh chain this run — now 3 sessions old across a full VIX round-trip AND an overnight risk-on gap-up. Est./carried.
attention.why: The flip is the calm/violent line — today's low, falling VIX and orderly gap are consistent with spot ABOVE the flip (positive-gamma dampening), which is what would let the gap hold rather than fully fade. But these levels are 3 days old across a vol round-trip and a gap, so they're the least trustworthy block; re-pull the chain in the gamma lab before sizing any level trade or reading the OPEX pin into 7/17.

recommendations.subject: Momentum complex (SPMO / VGT / QQQ): the 5-day-cross trigger is HITTING on the gap-up — scale in on the gap holding
recommendations.rationale: All three gapped up overnight (QQQ +1.34%, SPMO +2.88%, VGT +1.83%) with 5-day momentum healing to ~flat (QQQ −0.58, SPMO −0.27) and VGT's 5-day already CROSSED >0 (+0.31), the 21/63-day trend intact (+21 / +29 on 63d). The prior 'add on the 5-day cross' trigger is arriving. Execute on QQQ HOLDING its gap base + reclaiming its 50-day; scale, don't chase the print — a fresh gap invites a morning fade. Negate if the gap fully fills and the complex reverses red intraday. Size the three as one correlated factor bet (SPMO = most semis beta). Estimate, not advice.

recommendations.subject: Continue rotating FROM defensives (XLP just dropped out of Leading) toward tech + cyclicals (XLK / XLF / XLI)
recommendations.rationale: The leadership handoff broadened: XLP fell OUT of Leading (+0.5→−0.6) and XLY deepened to Lagging (−1.8) while XLK RS healed a 4th read (−0.53, near a 0-cross) and XLF/XLI/XLU strengthened — cyclicals + rate-sensitives taking the baton, not a pure defensive bid. Reduce the staples/discretionary tilt into strength; add to tech/cyclicals on confirmation. Trigger to complete the rotation: XLK 1m RS crossing >0 confirmed by QQQ holding the gap + a 50-day reclaim. Negate (stay defensive) if XLK rolls back below −0.6. Estimate from RRG + momentum-Δ, not advice.

recommendations.subject: Financials (XLF): RS +4.9 and rising, Leading — the cleanest cyclical into the 7/14 Q2 big-bank kickoff
recommendations.rationale: XLF 1m RS strengthened again (+4.2→+4.9), holding Leading while the other defensives faded — and CPI-week (7/14) is the Q2 big-bank earnings kickoff. A steepening/holding yield curve after the 30y auction plus solid bank prints is the presser for XLF. Add/hold; the trigger to press is XLF 1m RS holding >4 through the first bank reports, with the negate a break back under +3 as yields fall. Estimate, not advice.

recommendations.subject: Energy (XLE): RS collapsed to −6.05 (worst) — do NOT chase crude for trend; the CPI-tail hedge is shrinking
recommendations.rationale: XLE 1m RS deteriorated hardest (−4.2→−6.05) as the carried crude spike (~$74, STALE) appears to be fading — the energy tape is rolling over. Avoid/trim XLE for TREND; the long-energy 'headline-CPI hedge' is losing its rationale as the oil tail eases (a bullish read-through for duration/tech). Re-add only if WTI HOLDS a level AND XLE 1m RS crosses back >0. Re-verify WTI live — crude is carried, not fresh. Estimate from sector RS, not advice.

recommendations.subject: MSFT: diverged DOWN −1.8% overnight, 5-day rolled negative — the lone laggard; event trade into 7/29
recommendations.rationale: MSFT fell 383.34→376.53 (−1.78%) while the complex gapped up, its 5-day rolling NEGATIVE (+2.77→−2.02), 21-day still −8.55, 63-day fading to +0.81. It is the breadth divergence inside the tech complex — the RS heal is semis/broad-tech-led, not mega-cap software. Treat MSFT as a two-sided EVENT trade into the Jul-29 FOMC-day print (double catalyst), NOT a momentum long; watch whether its weakness spreads to the software cohort and caps the XLK RS cross. Implied move ~6% is STALE (Jul-6 straddle) — refresh live. Estimate, not advice.

recommendations.subject: Carry a defined hedge into today's auction → CPI 7/14 — VIX low (~16.7) so protection is CHEAP
recommendations.rationale: VIX ticked down to 16.74 as the tape gapped up, so downside protection is priced cheaply right as the near-term event density peaks: 8:30am claims + 1pm 30y auction TODAY, then CPI/PPI/retail 7/14-16 into OPEX 7/17. A favorable window to add/roll a defined-risk hedge that also covers a morning gap-fade. Term structure carried contango (IVTS ~0.85) = not panic-priced. SPY flip (~740, STALE 3 sessions) is the reference — re-pull the chain first. Estimate, not advice.

recommendations.subject: Gap-fill odds at the open: after a +1.3-2.9% overnight gap, the base to hold is the gap origin, not the print
recommendations.rationale: The complex gapped up meaningfully; in a low-VIX / positive-gamma regime gaps in the trend direction tend to hold/pin rather than fully fill, but a partial morning fade is the norm. Watch QQQ's gap base (~712, the 7/8 close) and its 50-day as the hold/fail line: holding the gap origin + reclaiming the 50-day confirms the leadership handoff; a full fade back through ~712 red is the 'exhaustion gap' negate. Re-pull the SPY gamma flip (STALE) to frame the level. Estimate, not advice.

psychology.read: REGIME: a risk-on GAP-UP within a calm, positive-gamma tape — the shakeout resolved up and is now EXTENDING, not just holding. NAME it: an orderly, dealer-dampened (positive-gamma) regime where the momentum/tech complex gapped up overnight (QQQ +1.34%, SPMO +2.88%, VGT +1.83%) with VIX ticking DOWN to 16.74 — a low-vol trend-continuation gap, not a fear spike. 5-day momentum healed to ~flat across the complex and VGT's 5-day CROSSED POSITIVE, so the 'add on the 5-day cross' trigger is arriving. The term structure is carried CONTANGO (IVTS ~0.85), consistent with the low, stable spot. CROWD POSTURE: a LEADERSHIP HANDOFF in progress — the market is unwinding its defensive hedge (XLP dropped OUT of Leading, XLY deepened to Lagging) and broadening into cyclicals + rate-sensitives (XLF +4.9, XLI +3.5, XLU +3.6 all strengthening) WHILE the deepest laggard, tech, heals a fourth straight read toward a positive RS cross (XLK 1m RS −6.15 pre-market → −0.53 now). Complacency is refilling (VIX 16.7) and early FOMO is showing in the overnight momentum chase. But it is NOT a clean all-clear: (1) MSFT DIVERGED DOWN −1.78%, its 5-day rolling negative — a breadth divergence inside the complex; (2) the gap itself invites a morning fade. Energy collapsing (XLE −6.05) reads as crude fading — a bullish falsifier that REMOVES the headline-CPI tail. FALSIFIERS: (1) the gap fully FILLS and the complex reverses red intraday (exhaustion gap); (2) XLK 1m RS rolling back below −0.6 (handoff fails); (3) a 30y auction TAIL today or a hot June CPI (7/14) re-firing the tech de-rate; (4) VIX term structure inverting to BACKWARDATION (IVTS >1.0). The read leans 'leadership handoff to tech/cyclicals underway' over 'exhaustion gap to fade' — with MSFT's divergence + today's claims/auction the live two-sided risks.

psychology.tiltOnProbabilities: CPI (7/14) odds RE-BALANCED toward symmetric (28/44/28) this run: the fading oil tail (XLE collapse = crude-fade proxy) REMOVES the headline-CPI upward bias the prior runs carried, so hot and cool are now balanced around a modal in-line. Reflexively, with the crowd already leaning back toward tech (gap-up), a cool print meets a market positioned for it = a potent extension, while a hot print is the clean negater of the whole handoff. TODAY's 7/9 claims/30y auction held ~0.57 in-line as the base case (the tape already voted risk-on pre-auction), with the 30y tail the underrated 0.28 risk. OPEX pin held 0.55 as VIX ticked down (positive-gamma-friendly). MSFT miss shaded slightly heavier than the (stale) option-implied for sell-the-news asymmetry, reinforced by its fresh −1.78% divergence into the 7/29 FOMC collision. All labeled estimates seeded from STALE options (Jul-6) / unverified consensus / carried crude — re-seed live.

QQQ.status: $720.95 (GAPPED UP +1.34% off the 7/8 close 711.44); 5d −0.58 (healed hard from −3.39, a whisker from a positive cross), 21d +0.79, 63d +19.1. A full gap-fill back through ~712 red is the negate.

SPMO.status: $154.90 (GAPPED UP +2.88% off 150.57); 5d −0.27 (snapped back from −6.79 — the biggest heal in the book), 21d +4.91 (rising), 63d +27.83 (strongest trend in the watchlist). experimental.title: Leadership-rotation reversal detector: defensive-RS decay + lagging-group-RS recovery crossover (idea)
experimental.note: Method: track the SPREAD between the average 1m RS of the current Leading defensives (XLV/XLP/XLU/XLF) and the deepest Lagging growth group (XLK) run-over-run; flag a 'rotation reversal' when the defensive spread COMPRESSES/broadens for N consecutive reads WHILE the lagging group's RS climbs toward a 0 cross (this run: XLK RS healed a 4th read −6.15→−0.53 while staples XLP dropped OUT of Leading (+0.5→−0.6) and cyclicals XLF/XLI/XLU strengthened — a broadening, not a pure defensive fade). Base-rate the forward outcome: does the crossover precede a durable leadership handoff or a failed head-fake? Inputs: sector daily bars + the RRG-lite RS-momentum already in the Tier-A slice. No owning tool watches the two-sided crossover today; it drove this run's 'handoff to tech/cyclicals' read. Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: Overnight-gap continuation-vs-fade base rate: does a low-VIX trend gap hold or fill? (idea)
experimental.note: Method: tag pre-market sessions where the momentum complex gaps ≥+1% overnight WHILE VIX ticks DOWN (this run: QQQ +1.34%, SPMO +2.88%, VGT +1.83%, VIX 16.9→16.74 — a positive-gamma trend-continuation gap). Measure the open→close path: what fraction hold the gap base vs fully fill, conditioned on the SPY dealer-gamma sign (spot above flip = pin/hold bias). Build the base rate that turns 'the gap invites a fade' from folklore into a probability, with the gap origin (prior close) and the 50-day as the hold/fail lines. Inputs: watchlist + index pre-market vs prior-close bars (have), ^VIX at each window (have), SPY gamma flip from the gamma lab. Directly frames this run's key open-risk. Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: Mega-cap breadth-divergence detector: complex up, largest weight down (idea)
experimental.note: Method: flag when the momentum/tech complex advances (QQQ/VGT/SPMO green) WHILE the single largest constituent diverges the other way (this run: MSFT −1.78% and 5-day rolling negative while QQQ/VGT/SPMO gapped up). Quantify the divergence (name return − complex return, plus the name's RS vs the group) and base-rate the forward outcome: does the laggard mean-revert UP to the group (healthy broadening) or does the group roll over TO the laggard (a topping tell, since the biggest weight leads the cap-weighted index)? Inputs: watchlist member bars + index-weight table (MSFT ~4.7% SPX / ~9.9% QQQ). No owning tool watches the largest-weight-vs-complex divergence today; it is this run's main caveat to the gap-up handoff. Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: VIX term-structure regime flag: contango→backwardation cross (idea)
experimental.note: Method: track IVTS = VIX/VIX3M (and VIX9D/VIX) daily; flag the cross of IVTS through 1.0 (contango→backwardation) as a regime-change trigger, with day-count of the current regime and a base-rate study of forward SPX returns/realized-vol after each cross. This run's whole read hinges on the curve STAYING in contango (IVTS ~0.85 carried); backwardation is the named falsifier. Inputs: ^VIX/^VIX9D/^VIX3M series (add VIX9D/3M to rlData). No owning tool watches the curve cross today. Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: Rate-driven rotation gauge: XLK-vs-XLF RS overlaid on the 10y yield (idea)
experimental.note: Method: plot XLK/XLF (and XLK/XLV) relative-strength ratio against the 10y Treasury yield and its 5/20-day change; regress sector RS-momentum on d(10y) to quantify how rate-sensitive the current leadership rotation is, and flag when the correlation breaks (rotation losing its rate driver = tech re-entry — which XLK's 4-read RS heal this run signals). Inputs: XLK/XLF/XLV daily bars (have), ^TNX or 10y yield series (need to add to rlData), with TODAY's 7/9 30y auction as a live test. This run's thesis is explicitly rate+oil-driven — no owning tool ties sector RS to yields today. Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: Crude/geopolitical shock → energy RRG divergence monitor (idea)
experimental.note: Method: flag when a sector's trailing RRG cell diverges sharply from its recent driver — either a top daily performer stuck deeply Lagging, OR (this run) a sector collapsing on its 1m RS (XLE −4.2→−6.05, the worst mover) as its carried commodity driver fades — a trend-vs-event divergence. Overlay WTI level + its 5/20-day change on XLE relative-strength; a rolling-over XLE is a live PROXY that crude is fading (which eases the headline-CPI tail), and 'trend-follow long' only triggers when crude HOLDS a level AND 1m RS crosses back >0. Inputs: XLE + sector daily bars (have), CL/WTI series (need to add). Recurs whenever an oil move (up or down) decouples from the lagging energy tape and doubles as the CPI-tail read. Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: Double-catalyst vol event-study: FOMC-day + mega-cap earnings collision (idea)
experimental.note: Method: event-study base rates for days when an FOMC decision and a top-10 S&P name's earnings land together (Jul 29 = FOMC + MSFT) — measure realized-vs-implied vol, average |move|, and next-day drift vs single-catalyst days. Inputs: historical FOMC dates + mega-cap earnings dates + SPX/QQQ realized vol + straddle-implied. Quantifies the compounding this run flags qualitatively. Not yet built; promote if the pattern recurs across quarters.
